“I find I get along better with the construction workers and the cab drivers...The people who count in the world.”-Donald J. Trump, 1989
Well, that statement was true in 1989 and it’s still true today.

This Labor Day, it’s important to remember just how much we’ve accomplished for the American Worker under my administration’s critical America First agenda:

- We built the greatest economy the world had ever seen, and we’re going to do it again.
- In August, we created 1.37 MILLION jobs - the 4th largest jobs gain in our Nation’s HISTORY and 2.5x better than any month in the failed Obama-Biden Administration
- Unemployment DROPPED AGAIN
- The Dow Jones hit 29,000 - HISTORIC. It would CRASH under Joe Biden.
- The Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) has provided more than $525 BILLION in forgivable loans to over 5.2 MILLION American businesses.
- And finally, America has been put FIRST again.
The Great American Comeback is well underway, and we can’t risk throwing all of our unprecedented progress away under a corrupt Biden-Harris Administration.

name: Chabot for Congress Press
subject: ICYMI: Democrat Kate Schroder ‘Unethical’ Hiring Scheme Leads to Calls for Her Resignation
body:
Cincinnati Board of Health member Kate Schroder, who is running against Rep. Steve Chabot (R-OH), was allegedly involved in an “unethical” hiring sche
No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-z/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-r/) **ICYMI: Democrat Kate Schroder 'Unethical' Hiring Scheme Leads to Calls for Her Resignation**
Breitbart
Sean Moron
September 4, 2020
[https://www.breitbart.com/politics/2020/09/04/democrat-kate-schroder-unethical-hiring-scheme-leads-to-calls-for-her-resignation/](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-y/)Cincinnati Board of Health member Kate Schroder, who is running against Rep. Steve Chabot (R-OH), was allegedly involved in an "unethical" hiring scheme that led to calls from Chabot's campaign to call on her to resign from her post.Schroder hopes to unseat Chabot during the 2020 congressional elections. Chabot [defeated ](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-j/)Democrat Aftab Pureval by 4.4 percentage points during the 2018 midterm elections and serves as a key district for Republicans to hold if they wish to regain the House majority. Republicans need to retake a net 17 seats while holding districts such as Chabot's to achieve a majority in the House.The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) [listed ](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-t/)Schroder as one of its top recruits in its "Red to Blue" program to flip red districts in favor of the Democrats during the 2020 congressional elections.Even though the DCCC said Schroder has a "history of service and putting people first," she has been accused of participating in an unethical hiring scheme.In August 2018, the Cincinnati Board of Health was sued for allegedly violating state law, city ordinance, department policy, and ethics rules by hiring one of its own members as the department finance chair. A judge ruled that the hire violated ethics rules by hiring one of its own members.The Cincinnati Enquirer [wrote](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-i/) in December 2019:_A Hamilton County judge ruled in September that the board violated ethics rules in hiring one of its own members as department finance director. State law, city ordinance and department policy prohibit a member of a public oversight board from a job with the agency that is overseen. The judge 's order required the department to dismiss the new hire immediately and seek repayment of salary._The lawsuit put the city potentially on the hook for tens of thousands of dollars in legal fees.Schroder was a member of the city board of health and was reportedly involved in the interview process for the department's new finance chair."Robinson and Johnson made the cut to the final three candidates," the Enquirer's Anne Saker wrote. "Lichtenstein wrote a letter of recommendation for Robinson. Among the board members doing the job interview was Kate Schroder, now a candidate for Ohio's 1st District U.S. House seat."Further, Schroder released an op-ed in February calling on the country to prepare for the coronavirus outbreak in the United States.She [wrote](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-d/) that "it's not a question of whether the coronavirus (COVID-19) is coming to the United States, it's a question of when. And we need to be ready."However, despite her call for the country to prepare for the coronavirus, Schroder [missed ](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-h/)a Cincinnati Board of Health meeting three days before she released her op-ed. During that February 25 meeting, Steve Feagins, the director of the Hamilton County Board of Health, delivered a presentation on the "Wuhan Novel Coronavirus Epidemic."As the coronavirus pandemic started ramping up, the Board of Health [admitted](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-k/) it was running a $2.7 million deficit.Schroder's record at the Cincinnati Board of Health led to Chabot's campaign to call on the Ohio Democrat to resign from her post.Jon Conradi, the Chabot campaign spokesman, [said](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-u/) in a statement in August:_Kate Schroder 's explanation of her involvement in an unethical hiring scheme at the Cincinnati Board of Health raise only greater questions and red flags about what she knew and when she knew it. We already knew Schroder directly participated in a hiring scheme that a Hamilton County judge ruled was unethical and which drew lawsuits at significant cost to taxpayers, now we learn that Schroder participated in the final interview and selection process, knowing it was wrong.__The records and explanation Schroder provided to the media indicate she thought the hiring was a potential ethics issue and yet she proceeded to participate in and facilitate the process anyway. If Schroder knew the hiring process was ethically questionable, that begs the question, why did she continue to facilitate the hiring process that a Hamilton County judge ruled was unethical and which drew lawsuits for corruption costing taxpayers thousands of dollars?_ Chabot's campaign even released an ad in August discussing Schroder's record at the Cincinnati Board of Health."It is clear that Kate Schroder does not have the judgment to represent hardworking Ohioans in Congress nor to continue to serve on the Board of Health," Conradi [added ](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-o/)in his statement.### | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-b/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-a/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jkuhdydy-kujkulhluu-f/)
---|---|---

During that February 25 meeting, Steve Feagins, the director of the Hamilton County Board of Health, delivered a presentation on the "Wuhan Novel Coronavirus Epidemic."As the coronavirus pandemic started ramping up, the Board of Health it was running a $2.7 million deficit.Schroder's record at the Cincinnati Board of Health led to Chabot's campaign to call on the Ohio Democrat to resign from her post.Jon Conradi, the Chabot campaign spokesman, in a statement in August:Kate Schroder 's explanation of her involvement in an unethical hiring scheme at the Cincinnati Board of Health raise only greater questions and red flags about what she knew and when she knew it. We already knew Schroder directly participated in a hiring scheme that a Hamilton County judge ruled was unethical and which drew lawsuits at significant cost to taxpayers, now we learn that Schroder participated in the final interview and selection process, knowing it was wrong.The records and explanation Schroder provided to the media indicate she thought the hiring was a potential ethics issue and yet she proceeded to participate in and facilitate the process anyway. If Schroder knew the hiring process was ethically questionable, that begs the question, why did she continue to facilitate the hiring process that a Hamilton County judge ruled was unethical and which drew lawsuits for corruption costing taxpayers thousands of dollars?_ Chabot's campaign even released an ad in August discussing Schroder's record at the Cincinnati Board of Health."It is clear that Kate Schroder does not have the judgment to represent hardworking Ohioans in Congress nor to continue to serve on the Board of Health," Conradi his statement.### | | | ---|---|--- Chabot for Congress, P.O. Box 54461, Cincinnati, OH 45254

name: Kat Cammack
subject: Hard Work Pays Off
body:
Labor Day represents American values
---
| | | | | |
---
| Friend,
To me, Labor Day represents American values - the foundation that our Founding Fathers built that patriots like you and I fight to protect every day.
So many of you are an embodiment of the American dream, and living proof that hard work pays off. Growing up on the ranch, I remember learning at a young age that the days start early and end late, and that dirt on your face was a sign of a hard day's work.
Now as a small business owner, I see first hand that the more the government interferes with our lives, the worse off we are as Americans, and the further we are from true freedom.
As the socialist agenda tries to plague our country, I have never been prouder to see working class Americans like you fighting back for the country we love, and the freedoms we deserve.
Here in North Central Florida, we don't shy away from work. We know how to pull ourselves up by our bootstraps, and we know that the American Dream that so many come to this country in search of is achievable with a little grit and a lot of hard work.
Labor Day is a day to look back at where we've come from, and the hard work it took to get us here, while also looking towards the future and focusing on what we can do today to ensure economic stability and freedom NEVER becoming a thing of the past.
I am grateful for every hardworking Floridian's remarkable efforts to make our State the best place to live and work, and I look forward to continuing to fight for you in Congress to make our future even brighter!
Happy Labor Day.
---
|
---
|
Kat Cammack Republican Nominee for Congress, FL-03
---
|
---

name: Mondaire Jones
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_
body:
This year, it’s more important than ever to recognize American workers.
#

Friends,
Every year, Labor Day gives us an opportunity to stop and honor American workers. And this year, it’s more important than ever that we express our gratitude to the workers’ rights organizations that fight tirelessly to ensure fair and livable wages for all.
My grandfather was a janitor, and my grandmother cleaned homes. I was raised by a single mother who had to work multiple jobs just to put food on the table. I know the struggles facing our workers because I’ve lived them myself.
In a time like this, when nearly 18 million Americans are unemployed and millions more work full-time and still live below the poverty line, we’re reminded of how far we still have to go.
That’s why we must fight to ensure everyone has a right to a good-paying job with good benefits. We’re seeing right now what happens when workers don’t have paid sick leave — when they can’t afford to self-quarantine and keep their families safe.
That’s why we need programs like Medicare for All, so that we never again see millions of Americans losing their jobs and their health insurance in the process.
That’s why we must pass a Green New Deal, which will create tens of millions of good-paying, sustainable jobs, including thousands right here in our district.
And that’s why I support a $15 minimum wage, indexed to inflation, so that workers can earn a living wage from _one_ job, and not be forced to juggle multiple jobs in order to get by.
In Congress, I’ll be a champion for working people, and I’ll fight to make my mom, my grandparents, and you proud.
Enjoy the long weekend, and get some rest. Tomorrow, let’s get back to work to vote out the Occupant of the Oval Office and elect pro-worker Democrats up and down the ballot.
Thank you for your support,
Mondaire

clean_body: This year, it’s more important than ever to recognize American workers. Friends, Every year, Labor Day gives us an opportunity to stop and honor American workers. And this year, it’s more important than ever that we express our gratitude to the workers’ rights organizations that fight tirelessly to ensure fair and livable wages for all. My grandfather was a janitor, and my grandmother cleaned homes. I was raised by a single mother who had to work multiple jobs just to put food on the table. I know the struggles facing our workers because I’ve lived them myself. In a time like this, when nearly 18 million Americans are unemployed and millions more work full-time and still live below the poverty line, we’re reminded of how far we still have to go. That’s why we must fight to ensure everyone has a right to a good-paying job with good benefits. We’re seeing right now what happens when workers don’t have paid sick leave — when they can’t afford to self-quarantine and keep their families safe. That’s why we need programs like Medicare for All, so that we never again see millions of Americans losing their jobs and their health insurance in the process. That’s why we must pass a Green New Deal, which will create tens of millions of good-paying, sustainable jobs, including thousands right here in our district. And that’s why I support a $15 minimum wage, indexed to inflation, so that workers can earn a living wage from one job, and not be forced to juggle multiple jobs in order to get by. In Congress, I’ll be a champion for working people, and I’ll fight to make my mom, my grandparents, and you proud. Enjoy the long weekend, and get some rest. Tomorrow, let’s get back to work to vote out the Occupant of the Oval Office and elect pro-worker Democrats up and down the ballot. Thank you for your support, Mondaire = * | | | Paid for by Mondaire for Congress Mondaire For Congress P.O. | | Team ― Labor Day offers us the opportunity to reflect on our country’s labor movement and honor our workers who give so much every day. The past several months have highlighted some fundamental truths: _we rely on our essential workers now more than ever, and we still have so much more to do to guarantee fair labor rights for every worker._ [**I’m asking you today to join me in a call to fairly support our essential workers. Sign the petition below to voice your support!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/19040924/247750746/-1820647212?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Mjg4MzhjMy0xOGYxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyNtFtou8Tf2qUw2N9CVt6mckUjf7ZfWIvVLESLZFN0=&emci=8abad1d1-c3ee-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&emdi=528838c3-18f1-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&ceid=6233172) | | | [Support our essential workers >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/19040925/247750747/-1820647212?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Mjg4MzhjMy0xOGYxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyNtFtou8Tf2qUw2N9CVt6mckUjf7ZfWIvVLESLZFN0=&emci=8abad1d1-c3ee-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&emdi=528838c3-18f1-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&ceid=6233172)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Gov.=20Phil=20Scott?=
subject: Emerge Stronger
body:
First of all, thank you for the support you’ve given me over the years. |
---
| | [ ](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d88300ee89&e=19cebfe173)
---
| Friend,
---
| First of all, thank you for the support you’ve given me over the years.

As you are well aware, these are unprecedented times. Over the last six months, I have been solely focused on leading Vermont through this once-in-a-century challenge and doing the job you hired me to do. I’ve tracked the data closely, listened to the experts and followed the science. Every decision I’ve made has been in the best interest of our health, safety and economic future.

I’m so proud of how Vermonters have stepped up to support our neighbors and communities — and as a result, our state has been an example for others across the nation.

As we rebuild and recover, my priorities will continue to be:**Growing the economy, making Vermont more affordable and protecting the most vulnerable.** These principles have served us well over the last four years and we cannot afford to change course now.

I want to be upfront with you, while leading the state out of this crisis, campaigning will not be my highest priority. But unfortunately, we’re just a few weeks away from voting, and the stakes could not be higher. **[So, I’m asking for your support to ensure we keep moving in the right direction, as we confront COVID-19 and rebuild a stronger Vermont in its aftermath.](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=54a2a80ad6&e=19cebfe173) **

If you care about Vermont as much as I do, and I know you do, it’s critical we have the resources needed to win in November. [I’d be grateful for any amount you could spare.](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5f086795b9&e=19cebfe173)

I pledge to do my very best to provide you a steady hand on the wheel as we navigate this crisis, and work tirelessly to make sure we emerge stronger as a state.

Thank you again for your consideration, and your faith and trust in me.

Sincerely,

Phil Scott
---
| [DONATE](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2a37b9a42f&e=19cebfe173 "DONATE")

name: Josh Hicks
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
Today is Labor Day and, for many of us, this weekend will be a time to enjoy friendship, family, beer, and BBQ. But it’s also important that we remember this day is a celebration of the blood and sweat shed by men and women across this country, who fought for fair working conditions, fair wages, and fair treatment.

|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/19041151/247753492/-1191128446?refcode=fl_email_fr_20200907LaborDay_JH_all_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODllNjRkYy0xYWYxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xrK7kgJzjsvhxikLoH8TPDDHAgym7od4kPiabbsIU-k=)
| | Friends, Today is Labor Day, and for many of us, this weekend will be a time to enjoy friendship, family, beer, and BBQ. However, it’s also important that we remember Labor Day is a celebration of the blood and sweat shed by men and women across this country, who fought for fair working conditions, fair wages, and fair treatment. So while enjoying that hot dog or that union-made beer, I’ll also be reminded that it was a collective effort of working people around this country who came together to say: We will be treated fairly for the work we do. I hope you have a happy, healthy, and safe Labor Day and, if you know a union worker or local union organization, make sure to thank them for the important work that they do. | | Dear friend, This Labor Day, during these unprecedented times, it is more important than ever for us to reaffirm our support for safe labor practices and working conditions. To the frontline responders, teachers, nurses, grocery store workers--everyone working so diligently to make sure we get through this pandemic safely and with minimal disruption: we see you and we stand with you. We recommit ourselves today to ensuring the protections and resources all of Minnesota's workers need to remain safe and to prosper -- now and in the future. Together, we will continue to advocate for good paying jobs and the rights of workers to collectively bargain for a better life. Happy Labor Day. name: Mary & the TNDP Team
subject: Celebrate the Best of Us: Celebrate Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Copy%20of%20Copy%20of%20CELEBRATE600w.png]
Dear Peter,
Happy Labor Day! Today, the Tennessee Democratic Party asks you to join us as we celebrate the working families of Tennessee and the labor unions who have been the collective voice that ensures American prosperity extends to the working families who make prosperity possible.
Today we celebrate the working families of Tennessee who sacrifice so much to teach our children, keep our bridges and roads safe, respond to 911 calls, serve our food and bus our dishes, steer our public transportation, keep the economy humming by rail, air, and road, build our houses and highrises, and so much more.
Today we also celebrate the labor unions who created the middle class by steadfastly proclaiming that the economy works best when there is a positive, partner relationship between labor and management. A partnership to ensure workers have a seat at the table to speak on their own behalf and management can be held accountability for unjust, unsafe, and inequitable workplaces and practices.
Today we celebrate labor unions who ensure that every worker - no matter who you are, what you look like, where you live, how you pray, or who you love - has unmatched economic opportunity and a reason to feel good about the future.
Unions are why working people earn a good living and have a better life. Unions make it easier to get the training needed to get good work. Unions make sure that those who already have good work are paid for their work. Unions help to create a growing economy that creates more good jobs. Unions won working families weekends off, vacations, sick leave, social security, minimum wage, anti-discrimination laws, the eight-hour work day and 40-hour work week, overtime pay, child protection labor laws, pensions for a secure and dignified retirement, collective bargaining rights, whistleblower protections, privacy rights, age discrimination laws, and parental leave.
The Tennessee Democratic Party is proud to uplift working families and our labor union brothers and sisters. This November, you can support them, too, by electing more leaders who will govern for the well-being of working families instead of treating the best among us as expendable. Check out our slate of Democratic candidates running up and down the ballot. [https://tndp.org/2020-democratic-candidates/]
VOTER REGISTRATION DEADLINE: Monday, October 5, 2020
EARLY VOTING: Wednesday, October 14, 2020 to Thursday, October 29, 2020*
REGISTER TO VOTE: iwillvote.com [http://iwillvote.com]
CHECK YOUR REGISTRATION: tnmap.tn.gov/voterlookup/ [https://tnmap.tn.gov/voterlookup/]
*It’s important for every voter to check their county election commission to confirm times and locations and to make a plan to VOTE EARLY DURING EARLY VOTING - it's safe, it's easy, it's quick!
Enjoy your day,
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/signature2.png]
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party
https://secure.ngpvan.com/oNKhpMgsTk-zA4pXE2fn4w2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oNKhpMgsTk-zA4pXE2fn4w2]
PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR. GEETA MCMILLAN, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.
Tennessee Democratic Party
319 Plus Park Blvd
Nashville, TN 37217
United States

subject: Organized Labor is the backbone of the middle class
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dani for Illinois
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/8c0805c66174/organized-labor-is-the-backbone-of-the-middle-class?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
Happy Labor Day - today marks the unofficial last day of summer and the start of the last stretch of the campaign! We owe so much to the Labor movement. It has given us things like safer workplaces, better wages, family and medical leave, breaks at work, and the 40-hour workweek. Today, I know that I’ll be reflecting on all that the Labor movement has done for working-class Americans and what is still left to do. With today being the unofficial first day of campaign season, **I am proud to report to you that my campaign is going in full steam ahead.** Our army of phonebankers are calling thousands of voters in the Illinois 16th every week, we are in the midst of a highly successful district-wide park tour, and our yard signs are popping up all over the district. [Can you chip in today to keep this momentum going into campaign season?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=c4487930d9&e=140a9b81e7) In honor of Labor Day, let’s talk about working Americans. I am in this race fighting for a $15 minimum wage, universal paid family leave, protections for unions, and banning so-called “Right to Work” laws. Why? Because I care about the people of this district. That is why I have been endorsed by countless unions, from the Illinois AFL-CIO to the Illinois Nurses Association, and they all recognize that I am on the side of working families. Meanwhile, my opponent’s record on working families is disappointing. He has voted against the Paycheck Fairness Act, in favor of Trump’s tax scam (which only benefited the wealthy), against raising the minimum wage, and against protections for workers in healthcare and social service. That is why the AFL-CIO has given him a lifetime rating of only 35%. **The people of this district deserve so much better, and we can do better. ** [**Click here to help us unseat an anti-labor, anti-working class Republican.**](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=e1162bac09&e=140a9b81e7) Dani Brzozowski Democratic nominee for Congress, IL-16

name: MJ Hegar
subject: My dad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
|

[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64891f89/7f51f7bc/42d57d2d/4efacfe0/2133887683/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter, I hope you're having a happy and safe Labor Day weekend. It's thanks to the tireless work of the labor movement that we have this day to celebrate. Even more importantly, we have the labor movement to thank for so many things we take for granted: the weekend, paid time off, workplace health and safety protections, and much more.

My stepdad, who raised me like his own, died in a workplace accident. If it weren't for the labor movement and unions across the country, many more families would suffer the same tragedy -- we wouldn't have the workers' rights, fair wages, or the safer workplaces that we have today. Every day, his death pushes me to fight for workers like him.

But Sen. Cornyn is fighting to undermine all that by working for special interests and DC bosses like Mitch McConnell -- not the hardworking families trying to put food on the table. We need to defeat Sen. Cornyn this November to ensure that we stand up for Texas families -- **[can I count on your help?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64891f89/7f51f7bc/42d57d2d/4efacfe7/2133887683/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjIwNjEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-finalcountdown?refcode=email_20200907.1nd__2061__&amount=10")**

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64891f89/7f51f7bc/42d57d2d/4efacfd9/2133887683/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjIwNjEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=2061&amount=10")**

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64891f89/7f51f7bc/42d57d2d/4efacfd8/2133887683/VEsHBQ/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. clean_body: | "" | | Peter, I hope you're having a happy and safe Labor Day weekend. It's thanks to the tireless work of the labor movement that we have this day to celebrate. Even more importantly, we have the labor movement to thank for so many things we take for granted: the weekend, paid time off, workplace health and safety protections, and much more. My stepdad, who raised me like his own, died in a workplace accident. If it weren't for the labor movement and unions across the country, many more families would suffer the same tragedy -- we wouldn't have the workers' rights, fair wages, or the safer workplaces that we have today. Every day, his death pushes me to fight for workers like him. But Sen. Cornyn is fighting to undermine all that by working for special interests and DC bosses like Mitch McConnell -- not the hardworking families trying to put food on the table. We need to defeat Sen. Cornyn this November to ensure that we stand up for Texas families -- "" This Labor Day, let 's support Texas workers with laws that make their lives safer on the job. | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: "" "" "" "" "" \-- MJ "" | | It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. | Dear Friend,

I want to wish Everyone in the 2nd District a Happy Labor Day!

Today, I am proud to announce that I have been endorsed by the United Auto Workers. This endorsement is particularly special to me because the UAW was founded in my birthplace, Detroit, and because the success of this nation was built on the contributions of the American worker.
---
| [STAND WITH UAW](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=eafe64b37f&e=5886ca081d "STAND WITH UAW")
---
| The UAW will be a major part of the economic revival this country needs. When the Biden-Harris Administration leads our country to building back better, American Workers will be on the front lines helping to foster the recovery.

I thank the UAW for their endorsement and continued support of our campaign. Their efforts, in combination with all the other labor endorsements I’ve received, will play a major role in our victory in November.

---

name: Gretchen Driskell

body:
| | | | **I STAND WITH LABOR**

**Today and everyday**

---
| [ADD YOUR NAME](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnn6M2L4lcb-awdC-BLNjuQ_ayumRiR517gT2cH3bb-guG6mnxenGfW_Ae9EUBRJnfN0AhsKifJVxcLDXo9iOaw_zLsMaUOt7N37XOFqhvS2fJehlzqM3SmaKHreHgLZfUsUai30IHhKQX8Vkb3-kByl-Eq8xL7MvEHkkvhXPS9vNMPBjzWfpGBsTni3iyuKiboaJdOliGuirqmS_QzPc2e-fzu5EJrkd935q3rRTw_hSd-cDJkm0Vwkbdsxe6ESkzE/34z/QH34G7tpQU2X75EPYolzYw/h0/v6y0Njf7zQZ0cscPwSnjEQAwH02oTbV1PSxxPNJSKd8)
---

The labor movement was built in Michigan and, while we still have a long way to go, without the sacrifices of dedicated workers, we would not have the protections in place that we do today.

**As we celebrate Labor Day today, I am committed to honoring their spirit. In Congress, I will fight each and every day for the rights of workers and to build an economy that works for everyone.**

**_[Will you join me today in standing in solidarity with the men and women in the labor movement?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnn6M2L4lcb-awdC-BLNjuQ_ayumRiR517gT2cH3bb-guA1VfyI2yaFIzrjB0kJsnfsZHX0wQDsRUAZzRArfoUPsmlfpLZt1tkgyAoiS3Zalubd4gyzWvWiQWzLNcEjVPV1H1x9S3aAB7sVsD7I4GB-RBbflLJn6zuQiCqwDcp_LhKqJ0s5dcSQY9pJnWzgo3jTxaDxua6yRA_VI3wgVZYYBVl4C5ajMV_aQxuTozpgPC7EB4eEO7VcCEuV17-vXWOo/34z/QH34G7tpQU2X75EPYolzYw/h1/lkIOKEJmJ3phDr1SdqNbC-UW4q4yv5PGTNgHpR-mTXI)_**

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnn6M2L4lcb-awdC-BLNjuQ_ayumRiR517gT2cH3bb-guHyogpmbuFLMqKncAGb-vJh6VsPjrXgCnLniykPAaJrgh6cJ06Kf9k6j_b9P7A8PwcjNYocJKu_-f_ZYbR8M1xNRWRAgC5SSyCtHXDVpHL16Qib5zQg-jTWwXZxapZHRBeKx9L-rT-E0r_3Glzx-acZE01Dq0teTGNrMZO2ZWbC80bpZGlFpG_51MKqg72WCXYBuf3r3eGZvBXA759FsKdk/34z/QH34G7tpQU2X75EPYolzYw/h2/Wu-MTvYAcwK2IF7f1UUZZHq1TuzFS6Pfgxo1HFcwGUc)
---

I honor the legacy of the labor movement today and everyday. It’s more important than ever that we stand together and fight for policies that help provide hardworking, middle-class families greater economic opportunity.

Please join me today in reflecting on the important role of the labor community in our nation’s history.

Wishing you a safe and happy Labor Day.

Thank you,

Gretchen

Paid for by Gretchen Driskell for Congress
---

As a nonpartisan mayor, Gretchen had to balance the budget, make tough decisions, and deliver results for people in our community. In Congress, she will bring that same leadership and start getting things done for all of us, here in Michigan.

name: Rita Hart
subject: Honoring the backbones of our middle class
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/LLfDQSYLMP2rssL8Ztzl3tVK9QSnXW8dCVHwbFIrltE=/nPMFizpvaXmGZApfXEeswzLDlh8BSOpY5eEyh64RsuY=?refcode=200907-email) Happy Labor Day! Today we honor America's workers and labor unions, the backbones of our middle class. Labor unions gave us the weekend, they set a federal minimum wage and have continually fought to improve working conditions for everyone. We must protect the right of workers and unions to collectively bargain for better wages, healthcare and paid sick leave. In the middle of a pandemic, we understand that the work of those on the front lines could not be more important or appreciated. From the grocery store clerks and sanitation workers, to nurses, doctors and teachers, today is a day to say thank you. There is so much hard work ahead of us as a nation, but I know how to roll up my sleeves and get the job done. Together, we can build an economy that respects work and works for all. Thank you,
Rita Hart **[P.S. If you want to elect a leader who stands with workers, not corporations and special interests, chip in $10 to my campaign today!](https://act.myngp.com/el/LLfDQSYLMP2rssL8Ztzl3tVK9QSnXW8dCVHwbFIrltE=/nPMFizpvaXmGZApfXEeswzLDlh8BSOpY5eEyh64RsuY=?refcode=200907-email&amount=10)** | ******_**Rita Hart is a lifelong Iowan, farmer, 20-year educator, former State Senator and candidate for Congress in Iowa 's 2nd Congressional District. name: Margaret Good
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/19044705/247825027/-940294256?refcode=090720-es-labor-day&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODYzM2U3OS0zMmYxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=y6xMf9TBv0PgzgFT_fvTvxBx-9W15fVKVflWXVuGmnE=&emci=d0690139-97f0-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&emdi=d8633e79-32f1-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74&ceid=9814644)

Friend,

Labor Day is not only a day of rest for workers across our nation, it’s a day of reflection and recognition for all the work we put in day after day to strengthen our communities and provide for our families. And we must also reflect and thank our brothers and sisters in the Labor Movement that brought us the 40-hour work week, the minimum wage, and critical protections for workers.

I want to thank all the workers here on the Suncoast, across Florida, and our nation that help keep things running. Especially our essential workers and our first responders that are on the front lines during this pandemic, risking their health to keep our economy moving and helping people in need.

Workers are the backbone of our economy, and should be treated with the respect they deserve. Unfortunately, that’s not always the case. That’s why I’m fighting to lift up workers and rebuild our economy from the ground up.

I will always advocate on behalf of workers, and if you can help me win this election, I will bring their fight to Congress and I will work as hard as you work every day to ensure workers are protected and supported in every way possible.

Thank you for supporting workers this Labor Day and everyday.

Margaret

PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
---
Margaret Good for Congress
PO Box 5073
Sarasota, FL 34277
United States
Margaret Good is a lawyer, runner, and state representative running in FL-16 to change the face of Southwest Florida, and we are so proud to have you on our team. Margaret is a strong voice for our values—she brings an incredible work ethic and common sense to her fight for working families, to protect Florida’s fragile environment, for accessible and affordable healthcare, and against the special interests that have hijacked our political process. We need to send her to Congress to continue fighting for us.
Email is the best way for us to keep you informed about what’s happening with the campaign, and we are excited to have your support! You may notice that we send a lot of emails asking for your support—that’s because we win when everyone does what they can to help. clean_body: Friend, Labor Day is not only a day of rest for workers across our nation, it’s a day of reflection and recognition for all the work we put in day after day to strengthen our communities and provide for our families. And we must also reflect and thank our brothers and sisters in the Labor Movement that brought us the 40-hour work week, the minimum wage, and critical protections for workers. I want to thank all the workers here on the Suncoast, across Florida, and our nation that help keep things running. Especially our essential workers and our first responders that are on the front lines during this pandemic, risking their health to keep our economy moving and helping people in need. Workers are the backbone of our economy, and should be treated with the respect they deserve. Unfortunately, that’s not always the case. That’s why I’m fighting to lift up workers and rebuild our economy from the ground up. I will always advocate on behalf of workers, and if you can help me win this election, I will bring their fight to Congress and I will work as hard as you work every day to ensure workers are protected and supported in every way possible. Thank you for supporting workers this Labor Day and everyday. Margaret PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS Margaret Good for Congress PO Box 5073 Sarasota, FL 34277 United States Margaret Good is a lawyer, runner, and state representative running in FL-16 to change the face of Southwest Florida, and we are so proud to have you on our team. Margaret is a strong voice for our values—she brings an incredible work ethic and common sense to her fight for working families, to protect Florida’s fragile environment, for accessible and affordable healthcare, and against the special interests that have hijacked our political process. We need to send her to Congress to continue fighting for us. Email is the best way for us to keep you informed about what’s happening with the campaign, and we are excited to have your support! name: Joe Biden
subject: Peter, Kamala and I will fight for workers and their families.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Labor Day is a time for all of us to remember exactly who it was that built this country. |
---
Peter,

Labor Day is a time for all of us to remember exactly who it was that built this country. It's a moment to reflect on the generations of sweat and sacrifice that unions and union workers put in -- earning the rights, the power, the wages, and the benefits that built and sustained the American middle class.

I've said it before, but it bears repeating -- Wall Street didn't build this country. Workers did. _Unions_ did. The very same folks who are holding our country together right now, and who have never once let America down.

But on top of the worst economic and jobs crisis since the Great Depression -- on top of President Trump's mismanagement of a pandemic that has turned being an American worker into life-or-death work -- there's an ongoing war on Labor's House. That's because the Donald Trumps of the world understand that if they diminish union power, they can run the table on every American worker, union and non-union.

It's just wrong. As millions of working families struggle because Donald Trump didn't have a plan to address this global health crisis, Republicans are only escalating their attacks against the workers and unions who are keeping us afloat -- even proposing a tax cut for corporations as they allow workers' unemployment to lapse.

Kamala and I know that building back better means building our economy around working families -- and that means raising the wages of workers and the power of unions. We know that educators, electrical workers, and bus drivers fuel the economy -- not CEOs and hedge fund managers. As President and Vice President, we will always stand on the side of working people. And by enacting transformational investments in America's future, we'll also create millions of good jobs that come with a fair choice to join a union.

We know we can't just go back to the way things were before the pandemic, Peter. We need to build an economy that rewards work, not wealth. We need to treat all American workers as essential not only in times of crisis, but every day.

That means not just praising workers, but _paying_ them. It means investing in America's hidden bedrock of caregivers, so that more Americans can join the workforce and earn the dignity and security that comes from landing a good union job. It means standing on the side of unions, so that more workers can live a middle class life and provide opportunity for their kids.

**So on this Labor Day, I want to thank all of our workers across America._You_ built this country, and if I have the honor of serving as President, I'm going to make sure that our economy serves _your_ needs, _your_ wellbeing, and _your_ aspirations.**

We see you, we respect you, we thank you, and we will fight for you.

Thanks again, and Happy Labor Day.
Joe Biden | [DONATE](https://go.joebiden.%3D%3D/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_sept2020?refcode=JB_EM_INFO_2020.09.07_B1_laborday-jrb_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=100&amounts=50,100,250,500&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_INFO_2020.09.07_B1_laborday-jrb_X__F1_S1_C1__ac")

---

Has the pandemic affected you personally, or do you just want to take a break from emails for a couple weeks? We understand. [**Click here to pause emails from the campaign for a two week period.**](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704335/289841258/VEsA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/Take-a-Break?source=footer")

Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email, with a check, or with a spouse -- thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If we got your name or any information wrong -- we're so sorry! To update and correct your information [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704334/289841258/VEsB/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/update-your-information"). If you would like to receive fewer emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704333/289841258/VEsO/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). If you would like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704332/289841258/VEsP/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/content/Unsubscribe").

Peter, thank you so much for supporting Joe Biden's Presidential campaign.

[ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704331/289841258/VEsHBQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer&refcode=email-bsd-20190515host-b20_FDR_states_NA-F1_S1_C1")

This isn't the time to be complacent. If you are ready to fight for the soul of this nation, you can start by donating to elect Joe Biden by clicking the button below.

| [DONATE TO ELECT JOE BIDEN »](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704330/289841258/VEsHBA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2019.08.07_B8_footer_X__F1_S1_C1__footer&amount=25")
---

We know we send a lot of emails, and we are sorry about that. The reason? We are relying on grassroots supporters like you (we're serious!).

But Peter, we don't want to bother you. If you'd like to only receive our most important emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704333/289841258/VEsHBw/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). If you'd only like to receive volunteer emails, [click here.](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/3570432f/289841258/VEsHBg/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/volunteer-emails") If you'd like to unsubscribe from our emails, you can [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/35704332/289841258/VEsHAQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/content/Unsubscribe").

To make a contribution by mail, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b206/1cd0ac28/15c443c59/3570432e/289841258/VEsHAA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://joebiden.com/contribute-by-mail/") for instructions.

We sincerely thank you for your help and support.
\- The entire Joe Biden for President team

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email
body:
# [#]
Peter,

I wanted to take this opportunity to wish you a Happy Labor Day. This Labor Day holds even more significance than in past years.

Today we thank and celebrate the essential workers who have tirelessly been working to support Americans these past few months, the healthcare workers who have put their lives on the line to fight this pandemic, and the postal service workers who have been delivering essential supplies and mail-in ballots to keep our democracy running.

Especially now, I want to thank our front-line workers that are fighting the devastating fires in California day in and day out. We have a lot to be grateful for today.

So I ask you to take a moment with me today to thank the workers across the country who have been fighting for us during these trying times. Let’s use today as a chance to reflect on the progress we’ve made and all the work that still needs to be done.

I hope you have a wonderful and safe Labor Day.

Mike Thompson
Authorized & Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/dd8c0da6-afee-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74/e8f6c332-38f1-ea11-99c3-00155d039e74?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDQ0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOGY2YzMzMi0zOGYxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xdJf4T90QPiZS8_iCtrtVeli8zovtibElON5lwsDNvY=&id=103862631 Mike Thompson for Congress

Napa, CA 94581

Today we thank and celebrate the essential workers who have tirelessly been working to support Americans these past few months, the healthcare workers who have put their lives on the line to fight this pandemic, and the postal service workers who have been delivering essential supplies and mail-in ballots to keep our democracy running. Especially now, I want to thank our front-line workers that are fighting the devastating fires in California day in and day out. We have a lot to be grateful for today. So I ask you to take a moment with me today to thank the workers across the country who have been fighting for us during these trying times. Let’s use today as a chance to reflect on the progress we’ve made and all the work that still needs to be done. I hope you have a wonderful and safe Labor Day. Mike Thompson Authorized & Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: Mike Thompson for Congress PO Box 10541 Napa, CA 94581 United States

name: Greg Stanton
subject: Happy Labor Day, From Our Team To You
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
body:
Friend,

I'd like to wish you and your family a safe and happy Labor Day. As you spend time with loved ones today, I encourage you to take a moment to reflect on the sacrifices, past and present, made by many in the workplace and through the labor movement to ensure workers receive adequate health and safety standards, an honest wage for an honest day's work, an 8 hour work day, paid sick leave, and other workplace rights.

Today, it's particularly important that we recognize essential workers - first responders, healthcare workers, and others who have responded to the pandemic with courage and sacrifice. Thank you for all that you've done for your communities during this critical time.

We're less than 60 days away from the election and every day counts. I need your help to make sure we continue to protect workers rights. Can you help me with a contribution before our important September 30th deadline?

Sincerely,

-- Greg

------------------------------------------
| Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
------------------------------------------

Phoenix AZ 85018 United States

name: Danny Friedman, Voto Latino
subject: Elections have consequences
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTO LATINO
body:
|
#
Your vote is your power, Peter. That is what my parents taught me. Before they became citizens, they always spoke reverently of the power of voting. After they became citizens, I went with them the first time they voted. They came with me when I voted for the first time. We don’t always agree on the issues, but we always vote together. **But to be honest, voting always felt kind of abstract — that is until I saw the difference voting could make in my home state.** In 2019 —**thanks to historically high voter turnout and participation** — the Virginia House of Delegates and State Senate changed from red to blue, with new legislators that represented the diversity of the Virginia population that had voted them into office. **We saw historically high voter turnout in the commonwealth of Virginia, especially among young people and people of color.** As a result, the Virginia General Assembly has passed bills that will male a real difference in people’s lives: raising the minimum wage, providing immigrants the chance to get a driver’s license, addressing the rise in hate crimes and white supremacist violence, establishing a no-excuse absentee voting, protecting reproductive rights, and even making election day a holiday! **The lesson from Virginia is that elections have consequences.** Yes, it can be draining to see the news on the federal level, but what keeps me hopeful is that Virginia is an example of what can be accomplished when we as a community mobilize. Now, the stakes are even higher. With Trump in the White House, it’s never been more important to empower voters all across the country to use their voice at the ballot box. Virginia is just an example, but this change can be made nationwide through registration, voting, and participation from our community. **But making sure that we are all registered takes a whole lot of resources.** [**Can you pitch in a donation of any amount to help us register one million young Latinx voters by election day this November? I promise your donation will go toward building a stronger and more inclusive democracy.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/19045965/247848832/-1577079102?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTVAvMS83ODUyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMjBlYWQ4My0zY2YxLWVhMTEtOTljMy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3Lcuq20G08YkYsGa32nZ2I6YYRlUQmiUnFDUh0Jn-k4=) When we all participate, we can see a government that looks more like us and treats our communities with dignity and purpose. We truly couldn’t do this without you. En solidaridad, Danny Friedman
Managing Director, Voto Latino
---
PAID FOR BY VOTO LATINO
---
Voto Latino
PO Box 35608
Washington, DC 20033
United States

subject: We need YOU...to write an LTE...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dani for Illinois
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/d7ee3113ec4a/we-need-youto-write-an-lte?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| | Hi everyone, I hope you're having a fabulous Labor Day!

As we get closer to Election Day and the campaign starts heating up (did you see that there will be a debate at the end of the month?!) we need all hands on deck. That means we need to make our support for Dani heard on social media, at events, and **in the newspaper**. This is where you come in.

In the next two months, we are kicking off a HUGE Letter to the Editor (LTE) writing campaign. **LTEs are the perfect way to get involved** , especially if phonebanking is not your thing, or you don't have enough time to commit to a big stack of postcards - we can do it together!

If you are interested - or think you might be interested - sign up [here](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=b59245e1df&e=140a9b81e7) and we will reach out. We have examples, drafts, and a big list of topics to choose from.

On to November!

Christianna Dyer
Digital Organizer, Team Dani

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: This is what we call building One Indiana for All
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We just released a new ad and we need your help getting as many Hoosiers eyes on it as possible. | |
---
| |
---|---|---
|
---
Friend,

We just released a new ad and we need your help getting as many Hoosiers eyes on it as possible.
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=0938f3f985164ff7ae7c25289adf1e37&_e=avhKOC6_4J4WzHZo9nsfQA3oPWlCzFjFcbB3u3tteS-4k5XGef4ntIROrrKKRmE1hLXx4jyH6ly_co--h9qI9SOvSlj2H-qPOEgsHFatJm_hykVWTIYreWZLKJTP8Jq5ZyuGvXnplwRpefjOJwckiP-95dcrOsiXF4u-H-M6jtKNtc3dJynO1wHI9rkwyzoEk1V5rqGTa9ubbDqdCtG9WOWEGGOs3k_oz7CarTTfTyDs81bbAC5OUp4f7bBk2cckFPeyh2Aa6Gm6sQm2vLAyDO5uExDpWKBW_TOWSWYE_i1qRVVKdWpgFz2De_cwcKMAEj0azpEon5TlHQJ-yMSH-JIjiR_AcSVHX3V4QbFTIQpY1o0TbErljnrbo9hHUbEoyQ1qWYSX1ezgXnsC5FWHGvZJGkO0R15FzAnT4Ot3qrq-WPhhQdv5jEsKN2v_NnLfk26z69nmtugRkO6re0qznlNx-A6hUpr6lQiqeHIHG3I%3D)
[**Watch and Share**](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=0938f3f985164ff7ae7c25289adf1e37&_e=avhKOC6_4J4WzHZo9nsfQA3oPWlCzFjFcbB3u3tteS-4k5XGef4ntIROrrKKRmE1hLXx4jyH6ly_co--h9qI9SOvSlj2H-qPOEgsHFatJm_hykVWTIYreWZLKJTP8Jq5ZyuGvXnplwRpefjOJwckiP-95dcrOsiXF4u-H-M6jtKNtc3dJynO1wHI9rkwyzoEk1V5rqGTa9ubbDqdCtG9WOWEGGOs3k_oz7CarTTfTyDs81bbAC5OUp4f7bBk2cckFPeyh2Aa6Gm6sQm2vLAyDMmKXWyM5XZAllFQoE9J6C0XmkTQbvsdNfx-oiUY-KMYn-u6GzISuuIdAF1AbFM298SrfCWZmtTUZpSDw5-pgxgnOrioWbr__u4Lw6FdKztaLlqeoAQQdvDGsSU3CJ06dY89jnLkANtY7Wx3l68gbuzxoWrQmGxpxnSGqtFH00Y7F-yZIDuauJ_fjU7MCdbL8o_w3ThKDJrlgcF4o83hpJo%3D)
Just a few years ago, the digital divide in the state was immense.

**Students were being hurt by the lack of internet access. Family farms were struggling to compete due to a lack of reliable broadband. Our seniors couldn’t access telehealth services that are so important today.**

_Our rural communities needed someone to step up and deliver for them. _

Governor Holcomb identified the problem as soon as he got into office, and started to work on a solution immediately.

He realized that words alone weren’t going to cut it and that decisive action was going to be required if we wanted to close the digital divide and make a difference for our rural communities.

_That’s why he devoted $100 million to building broadband infrastructure and partnered with local providers to connect communities and people across our state. _

**That’s what building One Indiana for All looks like. **

Friend, will you help us put our ad in front of as many Hoosier eyes as possible? Use the buttons below to share it with your friends, family, and neighbors on Facebook and Twitter.
[**Watch and Share**](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=0938f3f985164ff7ae7c25289adf1e37&_e=avhKOC6_4J4WzHZo9nsfQA3oPWlCzFjFcbB3u3tteS-4k5XGef4ntIROrrKKRmE1hLXx4jyH6ly_co--h9qI9SOvSlj2H-qPOEgsHFatJm_hykVWTIYreWZLKJTP8Jq5ZyuGvXnplwRpefjOJwckiP-95dcrOsiXF4u-H-M6jtKNtc3dJynO1wHI9rkwyzoEk1V5rqGTa9ubbDqdCtG9WOWEGGOs3k_oz7CarTTfTyDs81bbAC5OUp4f7bBk2cckFPeyh2Aa6Gm6sQm2vLAyDMaHyw1ivtJD3frnpcnrMkfcejWDB83mBBvv-MA3WBeT8KR3lrKywtfklrLnXTtF2sCWpjaLvkqRto6OWEKQWWFJMLTyFQntPiA1_akRNstOsCyNSlqAsDODiKIzdIcMKXU-Na_s8Kyhwg-fAvl8TkzMkK4akc-wuQTAtvoc6G8P5EQXgpklqxasMLQ5xX6ZqWda9vfDCL2ARMY1jka0JlA%3D)
Thank you for your support,

Holcomb Crew

name: Team Carney
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends for John Carney
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/9d_eHBlR1D1q1GvUrn6yvw==/FUAbrtlkbrO9uaajtvlEoA==?midqs=6nyPPlmAiTXrBzMhH78bhA==)
---

**FROM THE TEAM**

Happy Labor Day! We hope you and your family are having a safe and relaxing holiday weekend.

This holiday provides us an opportunity to reflect on the contributions of organized labor and Delaware workers who keep our state running day-in and day-out - especially during these difficult times. The nurses and construction workers, child care providers and law enforcement officers, teachers and grocery store clerks. Thank you for keeping our state moving forward.

Today and every day, Governor Carney remains focused on supporting Delaware workers and their families. **[Read more](https://act.myngp.com/el/9d_eHBlR1D1q1GvUrn6yvw==/lUraQYpKfUYZJPjNsZomKg==)** about the Governor's priorities to keep Delaware moving forward.

**IN THE NEWS**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/9d_eHBlR1D1q1GvUrn6yvw==/TqDNtuqlb8_qdEb60y0Q5w==?am=&recipientId=58ALV4wVN5uKbcFGD7pzR2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)

**Read more at WDEL:[ 'I'll always focus on things that need to be done' | Carney gears up for a 2nd term fight as Delaware's governor](https://act.myngp.com/el/9d_eHBlR1D1q1GvUrn6yvw==/2jIe6OFgC6qceoIuj_f8CQ==)**

Thanks for your continued support.

Team Carney

[**p.s. -- Our last fundraising deadline before the Democratic Primary on September 15 is TONIGHT at midnight. Can you pitch in $15?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/9d_eHBlR1D1q1GvUrn6yvw==/TqDNtuqlb8_qdEb60y0Q5w==?am=&recipientId=58ALV4wVN5uKbcFGD7pzR2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com)

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Honoring Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
Today's a federal holiday because on the eve of the 20th century, machinists, carpenters, and people who worked in mills, mines, factories, and more joined together to fight for better wages, better c | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28525188/45c30650/1c3016f2a/66e8c42e/2526060765/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Today's a federal holiday because on the eve of the 20th century, machinists, carpenters, and people who worked in mills, mines, factories, and more joined together to fight for better wages, better conditions, and a day to honor the power of working people.

Labor unions are still fighting for an America that works for everyone, not just the wealthy and well-connected. They were on the front lines then, and they are on the front lines now.

This year, many essential workers have been working in high-risk conditions without appropriate protective equipment, adequate safety standards, or basic job protections. So I'm going to keep pushing in Congress for an Essential Workers' Bill of Rights to ensure safe working conditions, fair pay, paid leave, health care security, support for child care, and more.

This public health and economic crisis has shone a light on all of the cracks in our economic system -- all of the traps that make it so hard for working people, especially people of color, to even get a toehold in the middle class. And those traps aren't there by accident. The rich and powerful have done everything they can to take power away from workers -- including trying to break the backs of unions.

But unions aren't going away -- not today, not tomorrow, not ever. Unions built America's middle class, and unions will rebuild America's middle class.

Throughout history, workers have gotten together and gotten organized. Thanks to the labor movement, kids went from working in factories to going to school. Americans went from working twelve hours a day, six days a week, to the 40-hour week we know now.

**We must remember that when working people fight, working people win. Shoulder to shoulder, we're going to create an economy that works for everyone. That starts with electing Democrats up and down the ballot this November -- and it continues with fighting for big, structural change in January and beyond.**

Thanks for being a part of this, and happy Labor Day,

Elizabeth

clean_body: Today's a federal holiday because on the eve of the 20th century, machinists, carpenters, and people who worked in mills, mines, factories, and more joined together to fight for better wages, better c | | | "" Today's a federal holiday because on the eve of the 20th century, machinists, carpenters, and people who worked in mills, mines, factories, and more joined together to fight for better wages, better conditions, and a day to honor the power of working people. Labor unions are still fighting for an America that works for everyone, not just the wealthy and well-connected. They were on the front lines then, and they are on the front lines now. This year, many essential workers have been working in high-risk conditions without appropriate protective equipment, adequate safety standards, or basic job protections. So I'm going to keep pushing in Congress for an Essential Workers' Bill of Rights to ensure safe working conditions, fair pay, paid leave, health care security, support for child care, and more. This public health and economic crisis has shone a light on all of the cracks in our economic system -- all of the traps that make it so hard for working people, especially people of color, to even get a toehold in the middle class. And those traps aren't there by accident. The rich and powerful have done everything they can to take power away from workers -- including trying to break the backs of unions. But unions aren't going away -- not today, not tomorrow, not ever. Unions built America's middle class, and unions will rebuild America's middle class. Throughout history, workers have gotten together and gotten organized. Thanks to the labor movement, kids went from working in factories to going to school. Americans went from working twelve hours a day, six days a week, to the 40-hour week we know now. We must remember that when working people fight, working people win. Shoulder to shoulder, we're going to create an economy that works for everyone. That starts with electing Democrats up and down the ballot this November -- and it continues with fighting for big, structural change in January and beyond. Thanks for being a part of this, and happy Labor Day, Elizabeth | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren Democrats All content (C) 2020 Warren Democrats, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

subject: An Urgent Request This Labor Day
body:
Working mothers need representation | | | |
---
| [Chip in ](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=8c35b8b182&e=3f151f8b74 "Chip in ")
---
|
---
| | | Friend, We’re working on borrowed time. With less than 60 days until election day, the time is now to make certain we’re giving mothers on the ballot all we’ve got!
Today, I want to call to mind the true meaning of Labor Day. On this day, we honor the tirelessness and tenacity of the backbone of this country: the working class. Without the labor movement and their continued fight for the rights of all workers, congressional candidates like Michelle De La Isla in Kansas and Elisa Cardnell of Texas might never have been able to run for office this year. We know how vast and varied the population of workers in this country is. Many are struggling single mothers who deserve policy that reflects and responds directly to their needs. The time is now to ensure their everyday battle receives tangible benefits. This can only be accomplished by putting more working mothers in office. **Will you join us in celebrating Labor Day by chipping in today? Every[$5](https://momsinoffice.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fb9d4118fda0058c977d045ec&id=dced24e263&e=3f151f8b74) helps us get closer to our goal.** Thanks so much for everything you do! Moms in Office

name: POST OFFICE SURPRISE (via Team Ben Ray)
subject: signature from 18326 needed
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by People for Ben
body:
---
| | [**USPS: We 'll deliver vote-by-mail ballots even without postage**](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs") [SIGN TO THANK THE POST OFFICE: The USPS just confirmed it will deliver vote-by-mail ballots even if folks can't afford postage! To show our thanks, we want to surprise the postal workers of 18326 with a thank you card signed by 20,000 of their strongest supporters. Will you sign the card and send them your thanks before midnight? >>](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs") Postal workers are risking their lives every day during this crisis.

Even though Trump's trying to bankrupt them -- the USPS is working tirelessly during this pandemic to deliver our live-saving supplies...

**[...and they just confirmed they'll deliver mail ballots no matter the case!](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsF/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs")**

**[_Will you sign the card before midnight and help us thank the USPS for protecting our democracy? We want to surprise them with 20,000 signatures. We know they 'll be so touched. >>_](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsC/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs")** | [](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsD/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs")
---
Despite the health risk of going into work...

Despite Trump's threats to shut down the post office and gut their jobs...

Postal workers know that we're facing the biggest vote-by-mail election of our lifetimes.

And they're going above the call of duty to deliver every single ballot -- even for those who can't afford postage!
[Will you sign the card and help show our appreciation to our postal workers? We need to gather 20,000 signatures by midnight tonight to pull off this surprise. Will you add your name before midnight and personally thank our frontline heroes for keeping our democracy running during this crisis? >>](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f689/2789135422/VEsA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/Thank-USPS-postcard?source=20200907_em_pet_b1_posv_30_rs")
Thank you!

Team Ben Ray

---
[](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68e/2789135422/VEsB/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.youtube.com/c/benraylujan") [](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68f/2789135422/VEsO/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.facebook.com/BenRayLujan") [](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68c/2789135422/VEsP/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://twitter.com/benraylujan") [](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68d/2789135422/VEsHBQ/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.instagram.com/benraylujan/?hl=en")
Born and raised on a small farm in Nambe, New Mexico, by a union ironworker and a public school employee, Ben Ray is running for Senate to build a better future for all families.

Ben Ray knows that our communities are stronger when everyone has a fair shot. He's dedicated his career to creating good-paying jobs for hardworking New Mexicans, expanding health care, and protecting our natural resources.

As Assistant Speaker in the U.S. House of Representatives, Ben Ray has worked to pass a people-first agenda that helps all Americans -- from lowering prescription drug prices to preventing gun violence, protecting our immigrant communities, improving equity in our criminal justice system, and removing the dark influence of money in politics. He is proud to be endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund, NARAL Pro-Choice America, AFSCME, American Nurses Association, League of Conservation Voters, Brady Campaign to End Gun Violence, Giffords, Human Rights Campaign, Latino Victory Fund, Congressional Black Caucus PAC, Council for a Livable World, and End Citizens United.

**Ben Ray doesn 't take a dollar from corporate PACs. Will you pitch in to fund our people-powered movement today?** **[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f682/2789135422/VEsHBA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/brl_email?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")** We know that we send you a lot of emails. If you would like to only receive the most important updates, [_click here_](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f683/2789135422/VEsHBw/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/fewer-emails").

If you would like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. You can keep up with our campaign by visiting us on _[Facebook](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f680/2789135422/VEsHBg/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.facebook.com/BenRayLujan/")_ or _[Twitter](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68c/2789135422/VEsHAQ/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://twitter.com/benraylujan")_ or _[Youtube](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f68e/2789135422/VEsHAA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.youtube.com/c/benraylujan")_ or online at _[benraylujan.com](www.benraylujan.com)_. Click [_here_](https://act.benraylujan.com/page/m/40f6b27d/18e32a7f/4a4eefde/7b20f681/2789135422/VEsHAw/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/unsubscribe") to unsubscribe and your request will be promptly processed.

People for Ben
PO Box 25371
Albuquerque, NM 87125

**[NOW:](https://act.eastmanforcongress.com/page/m/17c6e837/77d4c309/21af8c57/2635e534/2381868516/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiNSIsImUzdHpaV052Ym1SZllXMXZkVzUwZlgwPSI6IjI1IiwiZTN0MGFHbHlaRjloYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiNTAiLCJlM3RtYjNWeWRHaGZZVzF2ZFc1MGZYMD0iOiIxMDAiLCJlM3RtYVdaMGFGOWhiVzkxYm5SOWZRPT0iOiIyNTAifQ%3D%3D/?g=SoBiD8btZKdfO2weHdUSFCA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/eastmanforcongress-em?refcode=20200907_em_fr_b1_ehq_all&amount=5&amounts=25,50,100,250")** Kara needs to correct the record by reaching voters by mail -- _and she is SO CLOSE to raising the money to pull it off._ **[Peter, your donation has never been more impactful. [](https://act.eastmanforcongress.com/page/m/17c6e837/77d4c309/21af8c57/2635e537/2381868516/VEsB/?g=SoBiD8btZKdfO2weHdUSFCA "https://www.facebook.com/eastmanforcongress") [](https://act.eastmanforcongress.com/page/m/17c6e837/77d4c309/21af8c57/2635e536/2381868516/VEsO/?g=SoBiD8btZKdfO2weHdUSFCA "https://twitter.com/karaforcongress") [](https://act.eastmanforcongress.com/page/m/17c6e837/77d4c309/21af8c57/2635e531/2381868516/VEsP/?g=SoBiD8btZKdfO2weHdUSFCA "https://www.instagram.com/karaforcongress/")
Kara Eastman is running to flip Nebraska's second district from red to blue. She has dedicated her entire career to bettering her community through nonprofits and social work. She was inspired to run for office after her mom got cancer for the 5th time, and was prescribed a pill that cost $2500. Kara's mom chose to keep her house, and lost her battle with cancer after losing her life-savings to cancer drugs. As Kara watched her Republican Representative Don Bacon vote to repeal the Affordable Care Act and rob millions of their access to health care, she knew she couldn't sit on the sidelines.

If you want to support Kara's campaign for Congress, [_**please rush in a donation here. >>**_](https://act.eastmanforcongress.com/page/m/17c6e837/77d4c309/21af8c57/2635e530/2381868516/VEsHBQ/?g=SoBiD8btZKdfO2weHdUSFCA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/eastmanforcongress-em?refcode=em_footer&amount=25")

Another thing -- we know we send a lot of fundraising emails. It's because Kara has pledged not to take a cent of corporate PAC money. Unlike her Republican opponent, she relies on this grassroots community to fund her campaign. name: ALERT - via Jon Ossoff HQ
subject: Enough is enough
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JON OSSOFF FOR SENATE
body:
Trump and Perdue are out of touch |
---
Friend,

This should come as no surprise from Donald Trump's "favorite Senator," but **David Perdue is completely out of touch with Georgia's priorities**.

The pandemic has caused millions of Americans to be out of work -- including far too many Georgians.

And yet, David Perdue relentlessly fought AGAINST stimulus checks for working families and AGAINST emergency federal unemployment relief, _all while he fully supported billions for his corporate donors_.

**Friend, enough is enough. We need a leader who will help the people of Georgia -- not just his buddies.**

**[Can you chip in $20 to Jon Ossoff's campaign to help elect a Senator who will fight for a relief package and work in the interest of Georgia families?](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2a5a/5b2c1286/38d47680/25f08846/2666493210/VEsH/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_09_07_enuf?refcode=em_2020_09_07_enuf&amount=20")**
| | [DONATE $20 NOW >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2a5a/5b2c1286/38d47680/25f08846/2666493210/VEsE/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_09_07_enuf?refcode=em_2020_09_07_enuf&amount=20")
---

David Perdue and the GOP did not cause the pandemic.

_But the complete and utter failure to support working families and vulnerable populations through this unprecedented time falls completely on their shoulders.**That is their fault.**_

Perdue's record of helping his corporate donors and his own bank account while leaving Georgians to flounder on their own seals the deal: **He is not fit for public office.**

**[This is a challenging time for many folks, but if you can, please chip in a few bucks to help elect a new kind of leadership for this country.](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2a5a/5b2c1286/38d47680/25f08849/2666493210/VEsF/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_09_07_enuf?refcode=em_2020_09_07_enuf")**

clean_body: Trump and Perdue are out of touch | Friend, This should come as no surprise from Donald Trump's "favorite Senator," but David Perdue is completely out of touch with Georgia's priorities. The pandemic has caused millions of Americans to be out of work -- including far too many Georgians. And yet, David Perdue relentlessly fought AGAINST stimulus checks for working families and AGAINST emergency federal unemployment relief, all while he fully supported billions for his corporate donors. Friend, enough is enough. We need a leader who will help the people of Georgia -- not just his buddies. "" | | "" David Perdue and the GOP did not cause the pandemic. But the complete and utter failure to support working families and vulnerable populations through this unprecedented time falls completely on their shoulders.That is their fault. Perdue's record of helping his corporate donors and his own bank account while leaving Georgians to flounder on their own seals the deal: He is not fit for public office.
